task_completion.description: Navigate to the West Side Highway project in Redmine
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser shows the Redmine interface with 'West Side Highway' visible in the project header, title, or breadcrumbs.

task_completion.description: Apply a filter to show only open issues (not Closed)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Filters' section in the Redmine Issues list shows 'Status' set to 'open' or explicitly excluding 'Closed'/'Rejected'.

task_completion.description: Apply a filter to show only High or Urgent priority issues
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Filters' section in the Redmine Issues list shows a filter for 'Priority' set to include 'High' and 'Urgent'.

task_completion.description: Configure the issue list columns to include Subject, Status, Priority, Due date, and Assignee
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Options' panel in the Redmine Issues list shows exactly these columns in the 'Selected Columns' box, or the issue list table headers display these specific columns.

task_completion.description: Export the filtered issue list to a CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A CSV export options dialog is visible, or a browser download notification/popup shows a CSV file being downloaded.

task_completion.description: Save or move the exported CSV to /home/ga/Documents/critical_site_report.csv
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor shows the file 'critical_site_report.csv' located in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory. If the file contents are displayed, it should contain a header row and exactly 5 data rows.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with Redmine and configure the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Redmine web interface rather than using curl, API requests, or database queries in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent downloaded the CSV from Redmine rather than manually typing the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of a browser download or moving a file from ~/Downloads, rather than opening a text editor and manually typing out the CSV contents.

integrity.description: The agent did not extract data directly from the backend database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing sqlite3, psql, or mysql commands querying the Redmine database directly to generate the CSV.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target project is 'West Side Highway'. The exported CSV must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/critical_site_report.csv'. A perfectly correct export will contain exactly 5 data rows (plus the header row) because the synthetic environment contains exactly 5 issues matching the criteria (Status: not Closed/Rejected, Priority: High or Urgent). The required columns are Subject, Status, Priority, Due date, and Assignee.
